commentary_text: The model favors Australia at 35% while the market favors Turkey at 52%, a 19-percentage-point gap on the Turkey win. The draw sits between them: the model places it at 32% versus the market's 27%. The scale of disagreement on Turkey suggests the market is pricing in information the model cannot access—recent form swings, lineup changes, or injury developments among the squad since the model's last update. The dashboard flags this as a genuine signal of divergence rather than endorsement of either forecast.
